cluster_insights.description: Both insights indicate moderate volatility levels within the live cattle market, suggesting a correlation between volatility metrics and price trends.
supporting_insights: Live Cattle Future (front month) (April) shows ATR of 1.3693 indicating moderate volatility.
Live Cattle Future (third month) (August) has an ATR of 1.2529, indicating moderate fluctuations.
cluster_insights.significance: Understanding volatility can help traders better gauge the risk and potential reward in their investment strategies.

cluster_insights.description: The significant reduction in trading volume across both insights implies a cautious sentiment, indicating that low volumes reflect uncertainty among traders.
supporting_insights: Live Cattle Future (front month) (April) shows a trading volume of 10,020, below the average of 24,570.
Live Cattle Future (third month) (August) shows a trading volume of 9,775, below the average of 11,653.
cluster_insights.significance: Low trading volumes often precede important price movements; traders should be aware of this caution in their strategies.

cluster_insights.description: The positioning of current prices relative to support and resistance levels in both insights underscores the potential for price movement and reversal.
supporting_insights: Live Cattle Future (front month) (April) is near resistance (165.48) and support (164.1).
Live Cattle Future (third month) (August) price of 158.6 is near support (158.72) and resistance (160.25).
cluster_insights.significance: These levels can dictate trading behavior; understanding them helps traders anticipate potential market moves.

cluster_insights.description: Both insights reflect a similar bearish sentiment over the week, highlighting a trend that could affect trading strategies across cattle futures.
supporting_insights: Live Cattle Future (front month) (April) has 60% of days closing lower.
Live Cattle Future (third month) (August) also shows 60% of days closing lower.
cluster_insights.significance: Identifying prevailing market sentiment can guide traders in aligning their positions accordingly.

cluster_insights.description: Despite divergent trends, the April Feeder Cattle futures show a positive price trend in contrast to bearish sentiments in other markets.
supporting_insights: Feeder Cattle Future (second month) (April) shows a positive price increase of 1.4476%.
Feeder Cattle Future (third month) (May) has a negative weekly close-open change of -3.23.
cluster_insights.significance: This correlation highlights the potential for price divergence, suggesting that traders may need to adjust strategies based on the behavior of different futures months.

cluster_insights.description: Reduced trading volumes across different cattle futures indicate shared caution among investors.
supporting_insights: Trading volumes for March and April contracts are below average, reflecting reduced activity.
The trading volume of May has increased but still shows a bearish weekly sentiment.
cluster_insights.significance: This shared sentiment across contracts can indicate a broader market caution that may influence future trading behavior and price actions.

cluster_insights.description: There is a consistent bearish sentiment over the week for May futures, impacting trading strategies.
supporting_insights: Only 20% of days in May closed higher.
The other contracts also reflect cautious sentiment signs.
cluster_insights.significance: Market participants may need to exercise caution and evaluate potential risks due to the bearish sentiment observed.

cluster_insights.description: The cautious sentiment in the March contract is influencing bullish trends in April futures.
supporting_insights: March futures are showing reduced trading volume and moderate volatility.
April futures exhibit a strong bullish price momentum.
cluster_insights.significance: Understanding this influence can help traders anticipate potential shifts in market sentiment and pricing across futures months.
